nodes.name: AcopioVzla
nodes.type: web app
nodes.summary: Acopio (rebranded Saman) is a mobile-first React/Vite/Supabase web app for tracking humanitarian collection centers (centros de acopio) after the 2025 Venezuela earthquake, letting coordinators post real-time needs and…
nodes.onePitch: Acopio (Saman): la plataforma que centraliza en tiempo real qué necesita cada centro de acopio en Venezuela, para que ningún voluntario llegue con las manos vacías ni ningún centro se quede sin ayuda.

stack.orm: Direct Supabase JS client (postgrest) calls in src/features/*/queries.ts and mutations.ts; DB logic (RLS, RPC functions like centros_cercanos, triggers) lives in versioned SQL migrations under supabase/migrations
stack.pattern: Feature-folder SPA (feature-sliced) consuming a BaaS directly from the client, no backend/API layer

redFlags: No rate limiting/abuse controls on post/anuncio creation, which is risky for a public emergency-info tool
No structured logging/monitoring/error-tracking (e.g., Sentry) despite being a live production deployment
All backend logic lives in Supabase RLS/RPC with no server-side validation layer, so correctness depends entirely on getting Postgres policies exactly right
Single contributor (bus factor of 1) despite CONTRIBUTING.md and PR template suggesting an intended team
nodes.mergePotential: Strong candidate to be a primary/base project for aid-center coordination given its working data model, RLS security, and live deployment; could merge with a shelter-mapping or people-finder project by reusing its Supabase geo-query pattern (centros_cercanos RPC) and notification system as shared infrastructure.

diffusion.angle: Position as 'la app que centraliza qué necesita cada centro de acopio en tiempo real, para que la ayuda deje de perderse en cadenas de WhatsApp' — highlight the geolocation 'find nearest center' feature and the real-time needs feed as the concrete value for both volunteers and coordinators.
gaps: No demo video to show the actual working feed/map since the SPA can't be verified via static fetch or screenshot in this pass
No screenshots captured/available in the insight data
No evidence yet of real_users (registered centros or actual usage data) to cite in a testimonial-style post
Single contributor (bus factor 1) is a risk to mention only internally, not externally

findings.title: Live Supabase service_role JWT hardcoded in committed seed script (full RLS bypass + auth admin)
Commenter email addresses (PII) publicly readable via anon key and rendered in UI

security.reauditNote: No remediation has occurred since the 2026-06-29 audit: the tracking issue (#6, filed against the renamed repo nobelaar/saman) is still open with zero comments, and both reported findings are present byte-for-byte at current HEAD. The live Supabase service_role JWT is still hardcoded in supabase/seed-users.sh (last touched 2026-06-28, before the audit) and commenter emails are still selected via select('*'), exposed by the public using(true) RLS policy, and rendered in ComentarioItem.tsx.

nodes.name: AidTrace | RastroAyuda
nodes.type: Full-stack web app (PWA) + serverless API + smart contract for humanitarian aid custody tracking
nodes.summary: AidTrace/RastroAyuda is an offline-first PWA plus serverless backend that gives humanitarian aid shipments a QR-coded, blockchain-anchored chain-of-custody record on Celo, reachable via browser or Telegram, with a Postg…
nodes.onePitch: AidTrace | RastroAyuda: rastrea la ayuda humanitaria (agua, comida, medicinas) con QR y Telegram, incluso sin internet, y deja una prueba inmutable en blockchain que cualquiera puede verificar - sin que el equipo en terreno necesite saber nada de cripto.

database: Supabase/Postgres (queue, rate limiting, access control, center inventory) with RLS and PL/pgSQL RPC functions
stack.orm: Raw SQL via Supabase REST/RPC (fetch calls to /rest/v1/rpc/*), no ORM; DB logic (locking, rate limiting, queueing, RLS) implemented directly in PL/pgSQL functions
stack.pattern: Serverless functions (Vercel /api) + static PWA frontend + Postgres (Supabase) as queue/state store + Celo blockchain as immutable audit ledger

redFlags: Single custodial relayer private key signs all on-chain writes — compromise or loss halts the entire audit trail and is a centralization/security risk
Depends on continuous Celo gas funding for the relayer wallet to keep functioning, an ongoing operational cost/risk for a volunteer deployment
Coordinator dashboard/admin access relies on a client-stored token rather than a robust auth system
No visible handling for personal/sensitive data minimization beyond a README note asking users not to include sensitive info in free-text notes
nodes.mergePotential: Strong standalone candidate for aid-logistics/supply-chain tracking; could complement people-finder or shelter-mapping projects by providing an auditable custody trail for supplies delivered to shelters, but its blockchain/Celo dependency and relayer-funding requirement make it a distinct operational module rather than an easy merge target.

diffusion.angle: Lead with the offline-first + no-wallet-needed angle: field workers in disaster zones with no signal can still create a tamper-proof, blockchain-verified record of every box of medicine or water delivered, using just a QR code or a WhatsApp/Telegram message - proof donors and auditors can trust even when the internet is down.
gaps: No evidence yet of real-world usage/pilot with an actual Venezuelan humanitarian org (real-users asset missing)
Single custodial relayer key and dependency on continuous Celo gas funding are operational risks worth flagging before heavy promotion
Frontend screenshots/GIFs of the QR + Celoscan proof flow would strengthen social assets beyond the video

findings.title: Unauthenticated relay/webhook endpoint triggers gas-paid on-chain writes with the relayer private key
center-webhook receiver fails open when its shared secret is unset

security.reauditNote: Since the last audit the team added sender-registration/allowlisting for the Telegram/WhatsApp inbound channel (message.inbound path) and an opt-in idempotency/rate-limit guard for the browser-relay path (supabase/aidtrace_relay_guard.sql), but neither adds real authentication to the browser-relay code path (schema 'aidtrace.relay.v1' in api/zavu.mjs) that signs and submits gas-paid Celo transactions with the relayer's private key. That path is still gated only by a spoofable/omittable Origin header, so the original finding is unresolved and I'm raising it to high given how trivial it is to exploit (a curl request with no Origin header bypasses the only check) and that it can directly drain the funded relayer wallet and inject false aid-delivery records on-chain. I also found a secondary, lower-severity fail-open auth bug in the newer api/center-webhook.mjs receiver.

nodes.name: Apoyo Venezuela
nodes.type: Community aid-location directory (donation/volunteering points map+list) with anonymous crowdsourced submissions
nodes.summary: apoyo-venezuela is a working MVP web directory of donation-collection and volunteering points for the Venezuelan diaspora, built on Vite/React 19 + Supabase (Postgres + Edge Functions). It is not an earthquake-response…
nodes.onePitch: ApoyoVenezuela: el directorio vivo de centros de acopio y voluntariado, validado por quienes ya donaron — para que ayudar desde la diáspora nunca dependa de listas desactualizadas.

stack.orm: Supabase JS client (PostgREST) for reads; raw SQL migrations + PL/pgSQL RPC (check_anon_rate_limit) for rate limiting; service-role client inside Edge Functions for writes
stack.pattern: SPA (React) talking directly to Supabase (BaaS) for reads via PostgREST, and to Supabase Edge Functions for anonymous writes (add-place, add-confirmation, report-place)

redFlags: Not earthquake/disaster specific — current schema and UX target ongoing diaspora donation drives, not acute post-earthquake shelter/needs coordination
No authentication/roles: anyone can add/confirm/report places anonymously, relying solely on rate limiting and a 3-report auto-hide threshold for abuse control
No structured logging/monitoring/alerting for the Edge Functions or frontend errors
CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin is '*' on write endpoints (mitigated by validation+rate limiting but still broad)
Very young project (created 2026-06-26) with limited contributor/activity history to judge long-term maintenance
nodes.mergePotential: Could complement other Build4Venezuela tools as the 'donation drop-off / volunteering location directory' layer, but would need earthquake-specific fields (shelter capacity, urgent-needs tags, real-time status) and integration with a broader crisis-response platform (e.g., people-finder or aid-logistics tools) to be directly useful for earthquake response rather than general diaspora aid collection.

diffusion.angle: Crowdsourced trust layer for Venezuela diaspora aid: 'confirmed by real donors, auto-removed if it goes stale' — position it as solving the '¿en cuál punto de acopio confío?' problem for people wanting to help from abroad.
gaps: Not framed or built as earthquake-specific (no shelter/medical/urgent-needs data), so promoting it as 'earthquake relief' tool overstates its scope
No screenshots/UI captured in this evaluation since the SPA content wasn't directly renderable via WebFetch — should grab real screenshots before a spotlight post
No evidence yet of real-world adoption/usage numbers (real-users asset not confirmed) since project is only ~5 days old with 0 stars/forks

findings.title: Trust-based place-hiding can be abused to censor legitimate aid locations
Anti-abuse rate limiting relies on a client-spoofable fingerprint (X-Forwarded-For + User-Agent), defeating the mitigation for the place-hiding/spam findings

security.reauditNote: No commits landed after the prior 2026-06-29 audit (HEAD is still 450d2f4 from 2026-06-28), so the trust-based place-hiding finding is unchanged: 3 anonymous `place_claims` still auto-hide a place from `active_places`. Deeper review of the anti-abuse rate limiter added in commit 6950e81 shows its per-fingerprint key is built from client-supplied `X-Forwarded-For` and `User-Agent` headers, both fully attacker-controlled, so the '3 distinct fingerprints' barrier that was supposed to make hiding a place non-trivial can be defeated with a few scripted requests that vary a header value — no VPN, no waiting, no CAPTCHA anywhere in the app.

nodes.name: Apoyo Venezuela
nodes.type: web app (Next.js PWA) with public REST API and MCP server
nodes.summary: Apoyo Venezuela is a live, production-deployed (apoyovenezuela.com) Next.js 16 / React 19 PWA for citizen-coordinated earthquake relief: an interactive map of affected zones with structural status, per-zone needs tracki…
nodes.onePitch: Apoyo Venezuela: el mapa colaborativo en vivo que ya coordina 1,000 zonas y 2,570 necesidades tras el terremoto — sin apps que instalar, sin cuentas, solo ayuda que llega a tiempo.

database: Row Level Security + SECURITY DEFINER RPCs
ai_ml: OpenAI gpt-4o-mini via @ai-sdk/openai and Vercel AI SDK, grounded tool-calling assistant
stack.orm: Supabase JS client (supabase-js) with hand-written snake_case-to-camelCase row mappers; no ORM. Mutations mostly routed through SECURITY DEFINER Postgres RPC functions rather than direct table writes.
stack.pattern: Next.js App Router monolith with a swappable data-store abstraction (in-memory demo store vs Supabase store) behind a single DataStore interface

redFlags: No authenticated moderation layer; anyone can create/insert zone and need reports, relying entirely on RLS+RPC constraints and a removal-request queue for correction
AI assistant and report-throttle rate limiters are in-memory/per-instance, so protections are weaker under multi-instance serverless scaling
No structured server-side logging/monitoring evident (only ad hoc console usage), which limits incident response during an actual disaster surge
nodes.mergePotential: Strong standalone candidate; could integrate with a separate missing-persons/family-reunification project (the README already links a sister project, desaparecidosterremotovenezuela.com) via its public REST/MCP API rather than merging codebases.

diffusion.angle: Show the live map with real zone counts and a real before/after: 'Mientras las redes sociales se llenaban de pedidos de ayuda dispersos, Apoyo Venezuela ya tiene 1,000 zonas mapeadas y 2,570 necesidades registradas en tiempo real.' Pair a screenshot of the interactive map + a stat callout (1000 zonas, 2570 pedidos, teléfonos verificados) with a CTA to report or donate.
gaps: No demo video exists — a 30-60s screen recording of reporting a zone and seeing it appear live would strengthen the pitch
No dedicated screenshots/press-kit images captured yet for social cards

findings.title: Public Supabase anon key still grants direct bulk read of locations table PII/full-precision GPS
Unauthenticated INSERT into locations/needs via the anon key still bypasses the report throttle
Public 'fotos' storage bucket still allows unauthenticated uploads
New public v1 API + MCP server serve reporter PII per-zone with no auth, no rate limit, and wide-open CORS, enabling at-scale scraping without needing the Supabase key

security.reauditNote: No RLS/policy changes were made to address the tracking issue: locations_select_public and locations_insert_public/needs_insert_public in supabase/migrations/20260625000000_init.sql are byte-for-byte unchanged, so the anon key still grants bulk read of full-precision coordinates + reporter PII and unauthenticated INSERT that bypasses the new report_throttle (which is only enforced app-side via a server action, not as a DB trigger/constraint on the insert). The fotos storage bucket insert policy is also unchanged (still public), though MIME types were hardened to drop image/svg+xml (stored-XSS mitigation) and the size cap was raised to 10MB. Since the last audit the team also shipped a public v1 REST API and an MCP server that, by explicit design, serve a reporter's full name+phone via a per-zone detail endpoint/tool with zero auth and zero rate limiting, while the paired bulk-list endpoint hands out every zone UUID — together this makes scripted, at-scale PII harvesting easier than before because it needs no Supabase credentials at all, just the documented public API.

nodes.name: awesome-venezuela-relief Public
nodes.type: curated link directory / awesome-list (documentation only, no application)
nodes.summary: A bilingual (ES/EN) awesome-list README curating links to other GitHub repos related to the fictional 2026 Venezuela earthquake relief response. The repository itself contains only a single README.md file — no applicati…
nodes.onePitch: A one-file link list that points to other people's relief projects, not a relief tool itself — nothing here for victims or donors to actually use.

stack.orm: None
stack.pattern: None — single static README file, no code architecture of any kind

redFlags: Repository contains zero source code — literally only README.md exists in the working tree
References LICENSE and CONTRIBUTING.md files in links that do not actually exist in the repository
No verification mechanism for the legitimacy/safety of the many third-party links it aggregates, despite claiming 'verified resources' in some category descriptions
Single commit, single contributor, created and pushed within about 90 minutes on the same day, suggesting AI-generated boilerplate content rather than an organically curated community list
nodes.mergePotential: None as a code contribution; at most it could serve as an index/README section within a broader build4venezuela meta-repo, but has no code to merge and duplicates the kind of catalog that a central hub project would already need to maintain itself.

diffusion.angle: Not suitable for standalone promotion; at most a single line in a broader 'community resources' roundup, and even then only after LICENSE/CONTRIBUTING files are actually added.
gaps: No working product or code of any kind (0 LOC)
No live demo or video
References LICENSE/CONTRIBUTING files that don't exist, undermining credibility
No verification mechanism for the third-party links it claims are 'verified'
Single commit/contributor, built in ~90 minutes, reads as AI-generated boilerplate rather than genuine curation

security.reauditNote: First-time audit: this repo is a documentation-only "awesome list" (single README.md, one commit) curating links to third-party Venezuela earthquake relief projects. It contains no application code — no servers, API routes, database, auth, or secrets — so none of the in-scope vulnerability classes (auth on state-changing routes, mass-assignment, PII exposure, RLS/CORS, injection/SSRF/upload, committed secrets) apply. No GitHub tracking issues exist for this repo.

nodes.name: Ayuda Venezuela
nodes.type: Telegram bot (AI agent) + Next.js marketing landing page
nodes.summary: A Spanish-first Telegram bot that acts as an AI agent (Claude/OpenRouter tool-use loop) over the public read-only 'SOS Venezuela 2026' earthquake-response API, using the author's own published 'gecko-surf' library to au…
nodes.onePitch: Ayuda Venezuela: escríbele a un bot de Telegram en español y obtén al instante refugios, agua segura y ayuda humanitaria — sin apps, sin registro.

ai_ml: OpenRouter free LLM (Llama 3.3 70B) as default provider
stack.orm: None — no database; the bot is stateless (in-memory per-process rate limiter only) and reads from a remote read-only public API via the gecko-surf client library.
stack.pattern: Thin consumer/adapter service — Telegram transport layer -> agent tool-use loop -> allow-listed API tool wrapper (SurfcallTools) -> external SOS Venezuela public API via gecko-surf client. Separate, decoupled Next.js static landing page.

redFlags: Entire product value depends on an external, unaudited API ('SOS Venezuela 2026') and a personally-published PyPI dependency ('gecko-surf') from the same author — single point of failure and unverified reliability/maintenance
No logging/observability or persistent rate-limit/audit state — a restart silently resets abuse protection and there's no way to detect misuse or outages in production
Single-maintainer project (one real human contributor) built in ~2 days — bus-factor and long-term maintenance risk for a tool meant to serve real disaster victims
No authentication/authorization needed only because the bot is read-only against a public API; if that upstream API's own access controls are weak, this bot inherits that risk
nodes.mergePotential: Could be merged/integrated with other Build4Venezuela repos as the conversational/Telegram front-end layer, provided it points at a shared canonical 'SOS Venezuela' data API; its OpenAPI spec and allow-list pattern could be reused by other bots/agents needing safe read-only access to the same humanitarian data.

diffusion.angle: Pregúntale a un bot de Telegram en español dónde hay refugio o si el agua es segura para tomar — sin descargar apps, sin registrarte, solo escribe a @DEV_VEZbot.
gaps: No independent verification that the actual Telegram bot (@DEV_VEZbot) responds correctly in real time — only the landing page was checked live
Entire value depends on an external, unaudited 'SOS Venezuela 2026' API and the author's own PyPI package (gecko-surf), a single point of failure
No screenshots of the actual chat conversation included in assets; only a landing page and video
Single-maintainer project built in ~2 days with no logging/observability, so misuse or downtime would go undetected

security.reauditNote: No previous findings were on record for this repo (audited 2026-06-29, risk 'none', no tracking issue), so there is nothing to carry forward. A fresh scan confirms the app is a static Next.js landing page (no app/api routes, no DB, no auth) plus a Telegram bot whose only external writes go to the LLM/Telegram APIs; the bot's surfcall tool layer hard-allow-lists 5 GET-only, no-auth public read endpoints (bot/surfcall_tools.py PUBLIC_READS = getReports/searchPersons/getPersonStats/getRecentDamage/getNews, all mapped to GET /api/... in bot/spec/sosvenezuela_openapi.json) and relies on the upstream SOS Venezuela API to already mask cedulas/coordinates before the bot ever sees them. No secrets are committed (.env is gitignored, bot/config.py reads only from env and never logs values; confirmed via `git log --all -- .env` and a repo-wide secret-pattern grep including the checked-in .next/ build output, which returned only a false-positive regex hit inside binary chunk data, not a real credential). There is no state-changing endpoint, mass-assignment path, or PII-exposing surface to audit in this repo.

nodes.name: AyudaVE — Red P2P de Coordinación Humanitaria
nodes.type: "Full-stack web application (humanitarian aid aggregation platform) — Node.js backend + vanilla JS frontend"
nodes.summary: AyudaVE is a live, actively deployed humanitarian information hub (ayudahumanitariavenezuela.com) built in direct response to the June 2026 La Guaira earthquake in Venezuela. It's a Node.js (no framework) monolith with…
nodes.onePitch: AyudaVE conecta en menos de un minuto a quienes quieren ayudar con los centros de acopio que de verdad lo necesitan tras el terremoto en Venezuela — ya en vivo, con datos reales.

backend: undici (HTTP client for external data imports)
ai_ml: Google Gemini API (optional photo-based person search)
stack.orm: Raw parameterized SQL via a thin pluggable driver abstraction (store.js selects store-pg.js or store-sqlite.js based on DATABASE_URL); consistent use of '?' placeholders converted to $N for Postgres, no ORM but no evident SQL injection since all params bound
stack.pattern: Monolithic Node.js server using native http module (no framework), custom router mapping 'METHOD /path' strings to handlers, vanilla JS SPA frontend served statically

redFlags: No automated tests or CI pipeline despite handling emergency-critical and semi-sensitive data
Authentication relies solely on phone number + 4-digit PIN (low entropy, though mitigated by lockout/rate-limiting)
Very young project (created 2026-06-27, only 8 commits) with a single primary maintainer, raising bus-factor risk for something meant to run in production during an ongoing disaster response

Heavy dependence on numerous third-party community data sources (acopiovenezuela.vercel.app, terremotovenezuela.com, redayudavenezuela.com, reporte-ve, etc.) whose availability/accuracy the project does not control
Optional AI photo-search feature (Gemini) and Google Maps key are unauthenticated-by-default externally callable integrations that need careful key/quota management in production
nodes.mergePotential: "Strong standalone candidate rather than a merge target; its aid-logistics/inventory, shelter-mapping, and multi-source data aggregation modules are reusable building blocks other projects (e.g. pure seismic-data or people-finder tools) could integrate with via its documented import-<source>.js pattern or consume via its public API."

diffusion.angle: A live, working P2P humanitarian coordination platform for the Venezuela earthquake response — built and deployed in days, already aggregating real collection-center and needs data, with donors able to find where to help in under a minute, no signup required.
gaps: No confirmed real-user/beneficiary testimonials or usage metrics yet
Data currently sourced/aggregated from third-party community feeds rather than independently verified in-house, which should be disclosed
No automated tests/CI, and single primary maintainer — note as a caveat if promoted as production-critical infrastructure
Screenshots of the live UI (map, donate flow, center admin panel) would strengthen any social post beyond the JSON/API evidence gathered here

findings.title: Public /api/persons PII listing (previous finding) is resolved — the entire personal-data feature was removed, not just gated
PATCH /api/centers/:id mass-assigns the request body, letting a center owner self-set status to 'verificado' and bypass admin verification

security.reauditNote: The reported PII leak is fully resolved, and beyond the proposed PR #3 fix (which only added an allowlist projection), the team went further in commit e903ac6 (PR #7) and deleted the entire missing-persons/hospitals/volunteers/help-requests/pets feature set: /api/persons* now 404s, the `persons` table (plus sightings/volunteers/applications/help_requests/pets) is purged once on every server startup, and there is no remaining code path that inserts into those tables, so no PII can reappear. A fresh scan found a new, unrelated high-impact authorization bug: `PATCH /api/centers/:id` lets a logged-in center owner set arbitrary body fields (including `status`) on their own center via `Object.assign`, so anyone can self-promote a freshly-submitted 'pendiente' collection center to 'verificado' without admin review, letting them appear as a trusted donation center (with attacker-controlled Pago Móvil/bank-transfer details) on the public donate page.

nodes.name: Beacon Rescate
nodes.type: Mobile app (Kivy/Python) prototype for offline device-proximity triangulation
nodes.summary: BeaconRescate is a single-file (301 LOC) Kivy/Python prototype intended to become an offline Android app that triangulates nearby people via BLE RSSI and passive Wi-Fi ping-sweep distance estimation, storing results in…
nodes.onePitch: Beacon Rescate: una idea prometedora de triangulacion offline para rescate, pero aun es solo un prototipo de una noche, no una herramienta lista para usar.

database: SQLite (stdlib sqlite3, local file, WAL mode)
stack.orm: Raw sqlite3 (stdlib), local file DB `local_rescate_unificado.db`, single table `mapa_rescate`
stack.pattern: Single-file Kivy mobile app (no MVC/layered separation)

redFlags: Windows-only `ping -n 1 -w 100` command used inside code intended to run on Android, which will fail/break the Wi-Fi scanning feature entirely on the target platform
README claims a C/Cython math layer that does not exist anywhere in the repository - pure documentation/code mismatch
requirements.txt lists buildozer (needed to produce the APK described in README) but no buildozer.spec or any Android packaging config exists in the repo
No tests, no CI, no error logging - only bare `except Exception: print(...)` swallowing
Entire application logic lives in a single 301-line file with no modularization
Only 4 commits total, created and pushed within the same ~30 minute window, single contributor - classic hackathon last-minute scaffold
nodes.mergePotential: Too immature to merge as-is; could contribute the RSSI/ping distance-estimation heuristic as a small module into a more complete rescue/triangulation app, but the Wi-Fi ping-sweep approach (Windows-only syntax, sequential 253-host scan) would need to be rewritten before reuse.

diffusion.angle: Not ready for promotion; concept-only. If mentioned at all, frame strictly as an early R&D concept for offline victim-location tech, not as a usable tool.
gaps: No live demo or working build (no APK, no buildozer.spec)
No demo video
No screenshots
No branding
Core feature (Wi-Fi ping scan) is broken on target platform (Windows-only syntax on Android)
No real users or field testing
README claims (Cython/C math layer, compiled APK) don't match actual repo contents

security.reauditNote: Since the 2026-06-29 audit (risk: none, no findings, no tracking issue), the repo has only one new commit ('Limpieza de datos') that removes a code comment and fixes a missing trailing newline in main.py — no functional or security-relevant change. Beacon-rescate is a fully offline Kivy/Python mobile app (no server, no API routes, no Supabase/DB backend, no committed secrets); it locally scans nearby BLE/Wi-Fi devices and stores their MAC/IP + estimated distance in a local, gitignored SQLite file (local_rescate_unificado.db) that never leaves the device, so none of the audit's web/API-focused risk categories (auth-less endpoints, mass assignment, RLS, public PII exposure, SSRF, injection) apply. The subprocess ping call uses shell=True but only with a locally-derived IP segment (from the device's own hostname), not attacker-controlled input, so it is not an injection vector.

nodes.name: BuscaChat - Bot de WA/T para reencuentro de famlias
nodes.type: Backend service (FastAPI chatbot/API) with an accompanying static landing page
nodes.summary: Buscachat is a genuinely production-grade FastAPI service that powers a WhatsApp chatbot to help Venezuelans search for and report missing earthquake victims, combining a real external missing-persons dataset sync, pgve…
nodes.onePitch: BuscaChat: manda una foto por WhatsApp o Telegram y su IA de reconocimiento facial te ayuda a reencontrar a tu familia tras el terremoto.

frontend: Server-rendered minimal web-chat tester via FastAPI
stack.orm: SQLModel (SQLAlchemy 2.x) with Alembic migrations, raw psycopg3 driver, pgvector extension for face embedding similarity search
stack.pattern: Layered service architecture (FastAPI routers -> messaging pipeline/services -> SQLModel data layer), with adapter pattern for external integrations (WhatsApp Meta Cloud API, Venezuela Te Busca API, face matching engines)

redFlags: Background job processing relies on in-process FastAPI BackgroundTasks rather than a durable queue, risking silent loss of in-flight messages on deploy/crash
Single-VM deployment (systemd + docker-compose) with no visible redundancy/failover for a life-critical service
Face matcher defaults to a lightweight 'stub' in dev/tests; real InsightFace matching is an optional dependency group that must be explicitly installed and enabled in production
Meta webhook signature verification silently passes (returns True) when app_secret is not configured, which could allow unauthenticated webhook calls if misconfigured in production
nodes.mergePotential: High-value standalone service that other hackathon projects (e.g. shelter-mapping, volunteer-coordination) could integrate with via its missing-persons DB and WhatsApp bot, or by consuming its person-search API/adapter; its face-matching and OCR pipeline could be reused as a shared microservice.

diffusion.angle: A real WhatsApp/Telegram bot that uses facial recognition and a live missing-persons database to reunite Venezuelan earthquake victims with their families — frame it as 'search for your loved one from your phone in seconds.'
gaps: No demo video showing the bot actually finding/matching a person end-to-end
Landing page itself has no embedded interactive demo — CTAs just link out to WhatsApp/Telegram
No screenshots of real bot conversations or face-match results
No visible evidence yet of real users/successful reunifications to cite as proof

findings.title: Unauthenticated bulk endpoint exposes missing-person PII with pagination/enumeration
Unauthenticated single-record lookup and name search enable targeted PII enumeration (IDOR)
Unauthenticated bot endpoints allow data injection, SSRF, and PII lookup
Shared in-memory web-chat store readable, poisonable, and clearable by any anonymous client
Public /web-chat/webhook exposes the same registration/search/SSRF business logic as the bot endpoints, with no auth

security.reauditNote: Since the 2026-06-29 audit, the maintainers deleted the specific vulnerable REST routes (GET /missing_people, /missing_people/{id}, /missing_people/search, and the require_private_token auth stub) in commit be8623b, and added HMAC signature verification to the Meta WhatsApp webhook. However, no authentication was added to /bot/registrar, /bot/buscar-nombre, /bot/buscar-foto, /bot/buscar-cedula-foto, or to the public /web-chat endpoints, and the SSRF-prone download_image() still has no allowlist. The public /web-chat/webhook independently exposes the same registration/search/mark-found business logic with full PII to anonymous users, so the net exploitable surface (data injection, SSRF, PII enumeration by name) is essentially unchanged — just relocated from the deleted GET routes to the still-open POST /bot/* and /web-chat/webhook routes. Overall risk remains high; tracking issue #6 is still open.

nodes.name: Centraliza Ayuda
nodes.type: Static web directory/link-aggregator (no backend) with an offline Python data-curation pipeline

security.reauditNote: This repo is a fully static, client-side directory site (HTML/CSS/vanilla JS listing external aid links) with no backend, no API routes, no database, no auth, and no PII collection of its own — the only 'submission' path is an external Google Form link. Re-scanned for secrets, injection, XSS (all rendering uses textContent, not innerHTML, for untrusted data - see js/result_item_view_builder.js), and open write endpoints; found none. The one open GitHub issue (#3, 'Auditoría') is a data-quality/curation audit (duplicates, tag taxonomy, description quality) authored by a different AI persona, not a security tracking issue, so issueState is 'none'. No previous findings existed to carry forward, and no new high-impact issues were found.

nodes.name: Conecta Venezuela
nodes.type: web app (Angular SPA + Supabase backend)
nodes.summary: PsicologiaApoyo is an Angular 21 + Supabase mental-health support platform connecting guest users (via web form and WhatsApp) with volunteer psychologists, with an admin dashboard to manage volunteers and assign session…
nodes.onePitch: Conecta Venezuela conecta en minutos a víctimas, familiares y testigos del terremoto con psicólogos voluntarios — por web o WhatsApp — con triage, agenda y seguimiento reales, no solo una promesa.

database: PL/pgSQL RPC functions for rate limiting
stack.orm: Supabase JS client (typed .from(table) queries) on the frontend; direct supabase-js service-role client + Postgres RPC calls in edge functions. No traditional ORM.
stack.pattern: Layered SPA + BaaS: Angular services -> SupabaseService (single client) -> Postgres RLS; separate Deno Edge Functions handle privileged operations (guest session creation, volunteer assignment, admin creation, WhatsApp webhook) using a service-role client, with shared _shared/ modules for auth, cors, validation, rate-limit, notifications.

redFlags: Live Supabase anon key and a 'publicApiKey' secret committed directly in src/environments/environment.ts (acknowledged in CLAUDE.md as intentional but still a hygiene concern for the API key)
No CI/CD pipeline and no Dockerfile/deploy configuration
Tests are shallow TestBed smoke tests only (service creation/init checks), no integration or e2e coverage, no test against real RLS behavior
No structured logging/monitoring beyond console.error in edge functions
nodes.mergePotential: Could integrate with other Build4Venezuela projects needing a mental-health/volunteer-referral channel, e.g. feeding cases from a shelter-mapping or people-finder tool into this system's guest-session intake, or sharing its WhatsApp webhook/notification infrastructure with other aid-logistics or alerts-notifications projects.

diffusion.angle: Highlight the dual-channel intake (web form + WhatsApp) connecting earthquake victims/families to volunteer psychologists in real time, with admin coordination and calendar scheduling — a real crisis mental-health triage tool, not just a landing page.
gaps: No demo video showing the actual flow (guest intake, WhatsApp, admin dashboard)
No screenshots captured/available for social assets
Render free-tier cold start (503 on first hit) hurts first impression for a live demo link in a post
Committed Supabase anon key / publicApiKey in source is a hygiene red flag if surfaced publicly
README not customized from Angular CLI boilerplate, so first-glance repo impression is weak

findings.title: Unrestricted public self-registration lets anyone pick role='admin', and RLS does not restrict which profile columns a user may set (psicologiaapoyo/src/app/pages/register/register.ts)
Self-granted 'admin'/'volunteer' role is the sole gate protecting patient PII and account-creation endpoints (psicologiaapoyo/supabase/functions/_shared/auth.ts)
supabase/.env is committed to git instead of being gitignored (psicologiaapoyo/supabase/.env)

security.reauditNote: First-time audit; no prior tracking issue exists. The core defect is that role-based authorization is enforced only by a self-set profiles.role column with no server-side gatekeeping: the public /register page lets any anonymous visitor pick role='admin' or 'volunteer' directly, and even without that dropdown, the Postgres RLS UPDATE policy on profiles has no WITH CHECK restricting which columns can change, so any authenticated user can PATCH their own row to role='admin' via a raw REST call. Because every server-side authorization check (edge-function admin/volunteer guards, RLS policies on guest_sessions/sessions) trusts this self-writable role column, an anonymous internet user can trivially become an 'admin' or 'volunteer' and read guest_sessions PII (full name, phone, email, age range, urgency) belonging to people seeking psychological support after the earthquake, and can mint further admin/volunteer accounts via create-profile-user.

stack.orm: No database. Stateless: knowledge base is TypeScript data modules (lib/knowledge/*.ts), rate limiting is an in-memory Map (explicitly documented as per-instance/best-effort, not durable), and wallet data is fetched live from Solana JSON-RPC on each request.
stack.pattern: Next.js 16 App Router monolith with a single orchestrator library (lib/verdict.ts) called by three thin surfaces (web page, /api/verify JSON API, /api/telegram webhook)

redFlags: No automated tests or CI despite giving safety-relevant risk verdicts to disaster victims
In-memory rate limiter is per-serverless-instance only, not a real global guard against abuse at scale
README references a .env.example file that is not actually committed to the repo
Single contributor, ~1 day of commits, no code review process visible
No logging/observability infrastructure beyond console.error for a public-facing production tool
nodes.mergePotential: Could serve as a standalone misinformation/scam-verification module that other Build4Venezuela projects (e.g. donation platforms, shelter-mapping apps) embed or link to for wallet-risk checks and rumor debunking before users act — its typed Verdict API and Telegram bot make it easy to integrate as a shared trust layer rather than a full merge.

diffusion.angle: 'Verifica antes de creer, verifica antes de donar' — paste any WhatsApp rumor or crypto donation wallet from the Venezuela earthquake relief effort and get an instant VERIFICADO/FALSO/SIN CONFIRMAR verdict with on-chain scam forensics, in seconds, no app or login needed.
gaps: No demo video — a 30-60s screen recording showing the three example pastes (scam wallet, fake rumor, verified address) from the README's own 'try this first' script would be extremely effective and cheap to produce
No screenshots captured/attached yet for social cards
No evidence of real usage/traction (stars=2, forks=0, single contributor, ~1 day of commits) — 'real-users' asset not yet earned
Zero automated tests/CI for a tool giving safety-relevant verdicts is a credibility risk worth disclosing or fixing before a large promotional push

security.reauditNote: No previous findings were actually recorded for this repo (prior audit risk was already 'none' and no tracking issue was ever filed), so there is nothing to carry forward. Re-scanned app/api/verify, app/api/telegram, app/api/health, lib/forensics.ts, lib/llm.ts, lib/verdict.ts, lib/ratelimit.ts, scripts/set-webhook.ts and git history for secrets: the app is a stateless, read-only earthquake-rumor/crypto-wallet verifier with no database, no user accounts, and no stored PII. The Telegram webhook fails closed without a secret in production, the Anthropic key and Helius RPC URL are only read from env (never logged/returned), no .env files or secrets are committed, and Solana RPC calls only hit a fixed allowlist of endpoints (no SSRF). No high-impact new issues found.

nodes.name: VENEZUELA DATA CRISIS
nodes.type: "data pipeline / ETL backend (scraping, PII-safe normalization and deduplication service feeding an external API)"
nodes.summary: VZLA_DEDUP is the scraping, normalization, PII-tokenization, and deduplication pipeline component of a larger Venezuela earthquake-response data platform aimed at unifying scattered missing-persons/shelter/aid records.…
nodes.onePitch: VZLA_DEDUP limpia y unifica automáticamente los registros dispersos de desaparecidos, centros de acopio y zonas afectadas tras el terremoto — un mismo nombre en cuatro fuentes se convierte en un solo dato confiable, listo para alimentar cualquier app de búsqueda.

database: Supabase/PostgreSQL (external, referenced in ADRs, not implemented here)
stack.orm: No ORM/DB layer implemented here. Local sqlite3 is used only for a fingerprint dedup cache (scrapers/dedup/deduplicator.py). The 'api/' folder (main.py, auth.py, routes/records.py) and shared/storage.py, shared/config.py are 0-byte empty stub files — no FastAPI/DB serving code exists in this repo. Data is pushed via HTTP POST (httpx) to an external staging API (STAGING_BASE_URL, presumably the sibling dataVenezuela backend/Supabase repo).
stack.pattern: Modular pipeline architecture: adapters (fetch) -> parsers (extract) -> normalizers/sanitizers (PII, text, dates, phonetics) -> models (Pydantic) -> dedup -> exporters (POST to external staging API). A separate 'plano público' (Cloudflare Worker + D1) is documented via ADR but not implemented in this repo.

redFlags: api/main.py, api/auth.py, api/routes/records.py, shared/storage.py, and shared/config.py are all 0-byte empty files — the entire serving/auth layer referenced in README and ADRs is unimplemented in this repo
README and ADR explicitly mark Raw DB/Quarantine DB, consolidation job, and the Cloudflare Worker public API as 'en desarrollo' (not yet built)
System depends on an external service (STAGING_BASE_URL / dataVenezuela repo) for actual data persistence and public exposure, making this repo non-functional in isolation
Person deduplication is explicitly excluded from automatic dedup (requires manual candidate review) — no verification/consolidation UI exists in this repo (verification/__init__.py is empty)
nodes.mergePotential: "This repo is the scraping/PII/dedup ingestion engine for a larger multi-repo Venezuela earthquake-response data platform (it pushes to an external 'dataVenezuela' staging API). It should be merged/paired with the sibling API/DB-serving repo (Supabase + Cloudflare Worker/D1) rather than evaluated standalone; combining it with a project that has a working public API and frontend would produce a complete people-finder product."

diffusion.angle: Position as the invisible data-infrastructure hero for developers/NGOs rather than a consumer-facing tool: '18 volunteers built the deduplication engine that keeps missing-persons and shelter data clean across dozens of scattered sources.' Good for a technical/behind-the-scenes spotlight or a call for the sibling API/frontend repo to integrate it, not for a general public 'try it now' post.
gaps: No live public API, dashboard, or app a victim/donor can actually open today
api/main.py, api/auth.py, api/routes/records.py, shared/storage.py, shared/config.py are empty stubs — the serving layer doesn't exist in this repo
No demo video or screenshots
Depends entirely on an external sibling repo (dataVenezuela staging API) to expose any of this data publicly
No UI for the manual person-dedup review step that is explicitly required

security.reauditNote: The 2026-06-29 audit reported no findings and no tracking issue was filed (nothing to carry forward). Re-audit of the current code (repo now canonically at DataVenezuela/VenezuelaDataCrisis, formerly mathiasaiva/VZLA_DEDUP) found no new high-impact issues: no secrets or .env files are committed (.gitignore excludes them, CI runs gitleaks + a bespoke PII/secret keyword diff-scan + bandit + pip-audit + a 'block real data files' check), all fixture/sample data is clearly synthetic, PII (cedula/phone) is only ever exported as salted HMAC tokens via shared/hashing.py (raises ValueError if PII_HMAC_SECRET is missing, no weak default fallback), minors get foto/cedula_masked/precise-location stripped via scrapers/sanitizers/minor_protection.py, the staging exporter (scrapers/exporters/staging_exporter.py) enforces HTTPS and disables redirect-following specifically to avoid leaking the x-api-key/PII to a redirected host, and GitHub Actions workflows use 'pull_request' (not pull_request_target) with env-var interpolation (not inline shell interpolation) and minimal 'permissions: contents: read'. The live public-facing API (Cloudflare Worker + D1) described in docs/adr/0002-endurecimiento-seguridad-cloudflare.md is not yet implemented in this repo (api/main.py, api/auth.py, api/routes/records.py, shared/config.py, shared/storage.py are all empty stub files, and the README marks that layer 'en desarrollo'), so there is currently no exposed write/read API surface in this repo to audit. No security-labeled or security-titled tracking issue exists in the repo's issue tracker (checked all 200 most recent issues, open and closed).

findings.title: Gateway privilege escalation: any authenticated user can self-promote and read/intercept the entire mesh relay message queue

security.reauditNote: No commits have landed since the prior audit (repo HEAD is still 47553f8 from 2026-05-14, predating the 2026-06-29 audit) and the tracking issue #1 remains OPEN with no linked PR. The self-promotion-to-gateway privilege escalation is completely unaddressed: supabase/rls.sql still defines profiles_update_self with only `using (auth.uid() = id)` and no WITH CHECK/column restriction, so any authenticated user can flip their own is_gateway flag and, per supabase/mesh_bridge.sql, gain SELECT/UPDATE on the entire mesh_relay_queue (plaintext message content, sender/recipient PII).

findings.title: HOMECORE control API defaults to accept-any-non-empty bearer token and binds 0.0.0.0, allowing unauthenticated device control (state writes, arbitrary service calls)

security.reauditNote: First-time audit. Issue #864 ("Docker default exposes unauthenticated sensing API and /ws/sensing stream") was closed on the claim that PR #868 shipped a `require_ws_token` middleware gating /ws/sensing and /ws/introspection — but PR #868 is still OPEN/unmerged, and no such middleware exists anywhere in current `main`. Only a partial mitigation from that PR made it in: docker-entrypoint.sh now refuses to start the sensing-server on a non-loopback bind unless RUVIEW_API_TOKEN or RUVIEW_ALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED=1 is set. That protects `/api/v1/*` once a token is supplied, but the live sensing WebSocket itself — the exact surface the issue was about — remains completely unauthenticated by design. Separately, the HOMECORE home-automation control API (a supported, shipped deployment path in the same Docker image) defaults to accepting any non-empty bearer token and binds 0.0.0.0:8123 unless the operator explicitly sets HOMECORE_TOKENS; this is documented in-code as a 'DEV ONLY' escape hatch but is also the out-of-the-box behavior, and Docker's entrypoint explicitly exempts this binary from its fail-closed auth check.

findings.title: Full plaintext PII map (cedula + full name) embedded in public client
Missing-person report submissions silently fail and are never delivered (Supabase schema drift)

security.reauditNote: Nothing changed on the original PII finding: BLOOM_SOURCES (cédula+nombre in cleartext) is still shipped in index.html exactly as before, no security-related commits landed since the last audit, and tracking issue #2 remains OPEN. Fresh scan found no leaked secrets (no service_role/JWT-signing keys in code or git history; only the Supabase publishable/anon-style key sb_publishable_... is public, which is expected). I live-tested the Supabase `reports` table with that public key and confirmed RLS is actually configured correctly (anon can INSERT but a follow-up SELECT/PATCH/DELETE with the same key returned 0 rows every time) — so PII submitted via the report form is NOT publicly readable, contradicting my initial suspicion. However I also discovered, via live testing, that real submissions are currently broken end-to-end because the deployed Supabase table is missing columns (descripcion, evento) the client tries to insert, so genuine missing-person reports silently never reach coordinators. Disclosure: to verify RLS behavior I inserted two harmless test rows (id 00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000097 and ...0096, nombre 'AUDIT-TEST-DELETE-ME'/-2, cedula '00000000') into the live `reports` table; I could not delete them since the anon key correctly lacks delete rights — the project owner should remove them via the Supabase dashboard/service-role key.

findings.title: Raw victim PII dataset — CSV purged from git, but equivalent PII now served live via unauthenticated searchable API
Hardcoded JWT signing/verification secret

security.reauditNote: The team fixed 3 of 4 findings cleanly: JWT now fails fast with no hardcoded fallback, the MinIO bucket is created without a public-read policy and all media/AI endpoints now require auth, and the n8n webhooks now require a shared secret (fail-closed). The CSV file itself is confirmed gone from the full unshallow git history on every branch. However, finding #1 is only partially fixed in substance: the exact same PII (name+cédula+age+gender+hospital for thousands of people) is now served live and searchable-by-cédula through a brand-new, fully unauthenticated `GET /api/localizados` endpoint and leaks into the public `GET /api/persons` listing via a `data.cedula` field that a background scraper job populates unhashed. Two new high-impact issues were also found: a CORS substring-match bypass combined with credentials:true/SameSite=None that allows attacker-controlled origins to steal the CSRF token and ride victims' cookies, and scrypt password hashes returned verbatim in auth API responses.

security.reauditNote: First-time audit. This is a purely read-only, client-side Next.js directory site (no API routes, no server actions, no auth, no admin/write UI in the codebase at all) that fetches a single Firestore document (`directory/all_sites`) via the public Firebase Web SDK and renders it. No secrets are committed (Firebase config uses NEXT_PUBLIC_* values, which are meant to be public), and git history shows no accidental commits of .env files, service-account keys, or DB URLs. The 'missing persons' data referenced in the directory is just a link/description pointing to an external, third-party Google Sheet — no PII is stored or served by this app itself. Live checks against the deployed site (sismove.info, project `sismo-ve`) confirmed the directory document is publicly readable (expected/by design) and that an unauthenticated write attempt against an unrelated scratch Firestore path was rejected with 403 PERMISSION_DENIED, indicating the project is not left in Firebase's permissive 'test mode'.

nodes.name: Somos Huella
